rationale: The cited paper focuses on forward-looking consumer behavior and dynamic choice modeling, and the citing paper also models purchase-time factors with a prospective-accounting mechanism in a two-stage choice framework, so a substantive conceptual link is plausible. However, the only match evidence is a single DOI reference, and there is no in-text citation context or explicit indication in the citing note that this specific study is a core method/theory source. Given the sparse evidence, it is not safe to drop but not strong enough to clearly keep.
